acceptance_targets: [ch17_f001] PASS if revised lines 61-65 and 173 state one matching calendar date, and later clock markers (including 10:30 and 11:42) progress forward without contradiction; FAIL if any explicit date/time conflict remains in those spans.

[ch17_f002] PASS if the revised line 7 description is continuity-compatible with Mara's established look and requires no reader inference of an untracked transformation; FAIL if a tied-back/knot-length description remains without explicit explanation.
[ch17_f003] PASS if revised line 437 no longer implies a completed role transition before chapter 18; FAIL if Beau is still presented as definitively "former" without clarifying context.

[ch17_f004] PASS if the revised diner arc includes at least one explicit setback that forces new action before final federal confirmation; FAIL if the sequence remains a mostly frictionless cascade of favorable calls.

[F001] Pass if the date spoken at the interview setup and the date spoken on livestream are identical and unambiguous in the revised lines; fail if two different dates remain in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5624-5738.

[F005] Pass if a reader can map each major observed wound from prior scenes to a described act in the revised confession without inference gaps; fail if revised lines still leave one or more major injuries unexplained.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines 61-65 and 173 state one matching calendar date, and later clock markers (including 10:30 and 11:42) progress forward without contradiction; FAIL if any explicit date/time conflict remains in those spans.
findings.problem: The chapter records conflicting calendar/date-time anchors for the same event chain: Ruth dates the statement as October 26, 2026, but Mara's live slug says October 12 at 8:14 a.m., then the diner scene continues with same-morning timestamps. This breaks chronology and evidentiary clarity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile all explicit date/time markers in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:61-65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:285, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:423 so the confession, livestream, diner follow-up, and DOJ call occur on one coherent timeline.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised line 7 description is continuity-compatible with Mara's established look and requires no reader inference of an untracked transformation; FAIL if a tied-back/knot-length description remains without explicit explanation.
findings.problem: Mara is described with hair long enough for a loose knot, which conflicts with the continuity-sheet canonical physical detail of close-cropped hair during this cycle window.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Mara's physical beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:7 to align with canon (close-cropped hair), or add explicit in-scene continuity context that accounts for appearance change without creating an off-page leap.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised line 437 no longer implies a completed role transition before chapter 18; FAIL if Beau is still presented as definitively "former" without clarifying context.
findings.problem: Beau self-labels as "former detective" in chapter 17, but continuity canon keeps him in-role through chapter 17 and moves his formal exit to chapter 18. This creates role-state drift.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust the status line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:437 so Beau's employment state is unambiguous and canon-consistent (or explicitly mark it as sarcasm while preserving in-role status).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised diner arc includes at least one explicit setback that forces new action before final federal confirmation; FAIL if the sequence remains a mostly frictionless cascade of favorable calls.
findings.problem: The diner sequence stacks rapid institutional wins (federal intake, emergency release, DOJ formalization) with minimal resistance, which compresses consequence and reduces causal struggle right after the chapter's strongest live-conflict scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework escalation beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:285-345, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:357-371, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:419-447 so at least one concrete obstacle (missing corroboration, legal pushback, safety complication, or chain problem) must be solved on-page before confirmation beats land.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the date spoken at the interview setup and the date spoken on livestream are identical and unambiguous in the revised lines; fail if two different dates remain in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5624-5738.
findings.problem: The same scene assigns two different calendar dates: the interview slate gives October 26, 2026, while the livestream slate gives October 12 at 8:14 a.m. This is a hard timeline contradiction inside one chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Unify the date references in the opening interview block and livestream block at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5624, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5626, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5630, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5738. Pick one date consistent with late-story sequencing (before the November court material in chapter 18) and carry that exact date through both callouts.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can map each major observed wound from prior scenes to a described act in the revised confession without inference gaps; fail if revised lines still leave one or more major injuries unexplained.
findings.problem: Confession mechanics do not reconcile with established forensic observations from earlier chapters. Voss and Fenn admissions describe tools/sequences that omit major observed injuries, leaving prosecution logic internally unstable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise confession sequencing in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5650, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5656, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5676, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5692 so each major injury pattern is causally accounted for. Either explicitly add the missing injury actions to Ruth's account or narrow her admissions and name where a second actor's actions begin.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5594 conveys Ruth's defensive state without "face stayed still/went still/went flat" language; fail if the default facial template remains.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book prose tic: default facial stillness/flatness appears across 4 chapters (1, 3, 10, 17). This chapter should replace its instance to preserve variation at a major confession entry point.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Ruth's entry beat at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5594 so emotional bracing is shown through a more specific physical or tactical behavior (breath cadence, grip pattern, delay action) rather than the still-face template.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
